evals.name: add-rule-to-fragment
evals.prompt: I want to route github.com to my Work Chrome profile via Yojam. Add the rule to my chezmoi-managed Yojam config and make it apply.
evals.expected_output: The agent edits the desired-mutations fragment at `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json` (NOT the live `~/Library/Application Support/Yojam/config.json`, NOT a captured source JSON file). It mints a stable UUID with `uuidgen` for the rule's `id` field (required by Yojam's decoder AND by the modify_ merge so subsequent applies don't append duplicates). Provides a sparse rule entry: `id`, `name`, `matchType`, `pattern`, `targetBundleId: com.google.Chrome`, `targetAppName: Chrome`. Optional fields like `enabled` (defaults true), `priority` (defaults 100), `rewriteRules` (defaults []) are omitted. Then runs `chezmoi apply` to merge into live. Does NOT use `chezmoi re-add` to capture from a hypothetical GUI edit (re-add doesn't round-trip the modify_ stub).
expectations: Edits `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json` (the fragment), NOT the live `~/Library/Application Support/Yojam/config.json` and NOT a captured-source JSON file
Mints a stable UUID for the rule (e.g. via `uuidgen`) and pins it in the fragment so the modify_ merge finds the rule on subsequent applies instead of appending duplicates
Sets `targetBundleId` to `com.google.Chrome` (reverse-DNS bundle ID), NOT a path like `/Applications/Google Chrome.app`
Provides a sparse rule entry — only the keys that differ from upstream defaults; does NOT pad with `enabled: true`, `priority: 100`, `rewriteRules: []`, etc.
Runs `chezmoi apply` (or `chezmoi diff` first) to merge the fragment into live
Does NOT recommend `chezmoi re-add` (the source is a `modify_` stub, not a captured file)
Does NOT suggest editing the live `~/Library/Application Support/Yojam/config.json` directly

evals.name: bisect-silent-disable-target-bundle-id
evals.prompt: I added a rule to my Yojam fragment that should route brave-browser links to /Applications/Brave Browser.app. After `chezmoi apply`, the rule isn't firing and the GUI shows it as disabled. The other rules I added at the same time work fine. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the import-time security pass in `SettingsStore.importJSON`, specifically the path-prefixed `targetBundleId` trigger that silently disables rules whose targetBundleId starts with `/`. Fix: switch to a reverse-DNS bundle identifier (e.g. `com.brave.Browser`) in the fragment AND add `"enabled": true` to that rule entry, because live already carries `enabled: false` from the prior security-disable and the deltas-only merge (`dict.update`) preserves keys absent in the fragment. Then `chezmoi apply` re-merges; Yojam re-imports, the security pass passes, the rule fires.
expectations: Identifies the import-time security pass as the cause (not a decoder issue, not a chezmoi issue)
Names the specific trigger: `targetBundleId.hasPrefix("/")` (or equivalent description of path-prefixed targetBundleId)
Cites `SettingsStore.importJSON` (or names the import-time security pass) as the source
Recommends switching the rule to use a reverse-DNS bundle identifier (com.brave.Browser) instead of an .app path

Recognizes that live carries `enabled: false` for this rule (security-disabled at import) and the deltas-only `dict.update` merge cannot reset it by omission — so the fragment entry must explicitly include `"enabled": true` to flip it back on at the next merge
Notes that the fix lives in the fragment (`home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json`), then `chezmoi apply`
Does NOT suggest the decoder is at fault, the cask install is broken, or the file format is wrong
Does NOT recommend hand-editing live's `enabled` flag (it would be re-disabled on the next import unless the fragment also overrides it)

evals.name: re-add-does-not-roundtrip
evals.prompt: I changed a Yojam setting in the GUI. To capture it back into source I ran `chezmoi re-add "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Yojam/config.json"`. Either it errored or it overwrote my modify_ stub with raw JSON. What's the right way to capture a GUI change?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies that `chezmoi re-add` doesn't work for the modify_ + fragment pattern: re-add captures the live target verbatim into source, but the source here is a `modify_config.json.tmpl` script, not a captured file. The right flow is to hand-edit the desired fragment at `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json` to add only the keys that should be portable, then `chezmoi apply` to merge. If the user did overwrite the modify_ stub, they restore it from git (`git restore home/Library/Application Support/Yojam/`).
expectations: Identifies that `chezmoi re-add` is incompatible with the `modify_` + fragment pattern (the source is a script, not a captured file)
Recommends hand-editing the fragment at `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json` instead
Calls out that the fragment should hold only the keys that should be portable across machines — not a full capture
If the modify_ stub got overwritten, recommends restoring it from git rather than rebuilding by hand
Does NOT recommend `chezmoi add --force` as a workaround (same problem)
Does NOT suggest renaming the source back to a plain `.json` file (loses the merge engine and re-introduces the full-capture noise the pattern exists to avoid)

evals.name: icloud-sync-conflict
evals.prompt: Every time I `chezmoi apply` my Yojam config, within a few seconds the file changes back to something different. I noticed I have iCloud sync enabled on Yojam across two Macs. How do I make chezmoi the source of truth?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the conflict between Yojam's iCloud KV sync and chezmoi ownership. Both are trying to be authoritative. Recommends adding `"iCloudSync": false` to the desired fragment at `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json`, then `chezmoi apply` so the merge writes iCloudSync=false into live, then turning iCloud sync OFF on the other Mac via the Yojam GUI (or by applying the same fragment there).
expectations: Identifies Yojam's iCloud sync as the source of the overwrites (not chezmoi misbehaving, not a watcher loop)
Names the `iCloudSync` field
Recommends adding `iCloudSync: false` to the desired fragment at `home/.chezmoiassets/yojam-config.json`
Recommends `chezmoi apply` to merge the false value into live
Suggests propagating to the other Mac via `chezmoi apply` rather than via iCloud
Does NOT suggest disabling chezmoi management of the file or moving it out of source
Does NOT suggest editing the live target directly or fighting the watcher with timing tricks

evals.name: onboarding-new-mac
evals.prompt: I just provisioned a new Mac with the dotfiles. Yojam is installed via the cask in packages.toml. How do I get my existing rule set onto this Mac?
evals.expected_output: The agent walks through: (1) `chezmoi apply` runs the modify_ stub against an empty live (no config.json yet), so its output is the desired fragment re-serialized through `json.dumps` with 2-space indent; (2) launch Yojam from /Applications and approve the macOS prompt to set Yojam as the default browser (Apple requires user confirmation for default-browser changes); (3) Yojam imports the fragment, fills upstream defaults, and auto-discovers browsers/email clients. Notes that opting a Mac out is via the cask-presence gate in `home/.chezmoiignore`.
expectations: Walks through the install -> chezmoi apply -> first launch -> default browser approval sequence
Notes that on a fresh Mac the modify_ stub runs against empty stdin and emits the fragment re-serialized through `json.dumps` (2-space indent), not literal-bytes verbatim
Notes that macOS requires user confirmation to change the default browser (so it's an interactive step, not automatable)
Mentions Yojam imports the file on launch; auto-discovery fills in browsers/email; defaults fill in everything else not in the fragment
Notes the .chezmoiignore cask-presence gate as the opt-out mechanism for hosts that should NOT inherit
Does NOT suggest hand-copying the live config.json from another Mac
Does NOT suggest using Yojam's iCloud sync as an alternative (chezmoi owns the file)

evals.name: bisect-silent-disable-rule-launch-args
evals.prompt: I added a Yojam rule to my fragment that opens Slack links with a custom launch argument so a specific Chrome window position is forced. The rule has `ruleCustomLaunchArgs: "--window-position=0,0 $URL"`. After `chezmoi apply`, Yojam's GUI shows the rule as disabled. The targetBundleId is the normal `com.google.Chrome` reverse-DNS form, so the path-prefix trigger doesn't apply. Why is it disabled and what's my fix?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the second rule-level security trigger in `SettingsStore.importJSON`: any non-null `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` causes silent disable, even with a clean targetBundleId. The fix is two steps because the merge cannot delete a key by omission: (1) drop `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` from the fragment AND add `"enabled": true` to the rule entry; (2) clear `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` out of live (delete the rule in the Yojam GUI so the next `chezmoi apply` re-creates it from the now-clean fragment, or `jq del` the field from `~/Library/Application Support/Yojam/config.json` before re-applying). The same trigger applies to BrowserEntry-level `customLaunchArgs`, so a dedicated browser entry doesn't help — Yojam treats both as code-execution vectors by design.
expectations: Identifies `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` non-null as the trigger (NOT path-prefixed targetBundleId, NOT the regex check, NOT a chezmoi issue)
Cites `SettingsStore.importJSON` (or names the import-time security pass) as the source
Recommends dropping `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` from the fragment as the realistic fix (or notes that the design intent is to refuse code-execution vectors via imported config)

Recognizes that the deltas-only `dict.update` merge cannot delete `ruleCustomLaunchArgs` from the live entry by omission — Yojam would re-trip the trigger on the next import — so the fix also requires clearing live (delete the rule in the Yojam GUI before re-applying, or `jq del` the field from live)
Adds `"enabled": true` to the fragment entry so the next merge flips live's `enabled: false` back on once the offending field is gone
If the agent suggests a dedicated BrowserEntry as a workaround, it must note that BrowserEntry-level `customLaunchArgs` triggers the same disable
Does NOT confuse this with the path-prefixed targetBundleId trigger covered in eval2
